mission.title: rook: CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher IP without Specifying Session ID
mission.description: CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher IP without Specifying Session ID. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your rook deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
**Is this a bug report or feature request?**
* Bug Report

**Deviation from expected behavior:**
In https://github.com/rook/rook/pull/16009, a feature was added that blocks the watcher IP of each image before moving it to trash to ensure Ceph will

steps.title: Apply the fix for CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks Watcher…
steps.description:
blocklisting IP can cause problems when the cleanup job pod runs on the same host or gets the same ip as that of the rbd client ip that is present in the rbd status/watchers. instead of blocklisting the whole ip of the node or the client blocklist
only the unique ip:nonce to allow the node to not
```yaml
2025-09-11 16:37:31.434476 D | exec: Running command: ceph rbd task add trash remove my-pool/my-namespace/cf64122559b990 --connect-timeout=15 --cluster=rook-ceph --conf=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/rook-ceph.config --name=client.admin --keyring=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/client.admin.keyring --format json
2025-09-11 16:37:31.785810 E | cleanup: failed to add task to remove image "csi-vol-456f3d06-cbf2-44ee-bb7b-c3fa01d3a884" from trash in cephblockpool "my-pool" in rados namespace "my-namespace": failed to delete image "cf64122559b990" in cephblockpool "my-pool" from trash: exit status 11
```

steps.title: Confirm CephBlockPoolRadosNamespace Force Deletion Blocks… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: blocklisting IP can cause problems when the cleanup job pod runs on the same host or gets the same ip as that of the rbd client ip that is present in the rbd status/watchers.

2025-09-11 16:37:31.434476 D | exec: Running command: ceph rbd task add trash remove my-pool/my-namespace/cf64122559b990 --connect-timeout=15 --cluster=rook-ceph --conf=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/rook-ceph.config --name=client.admin --keyring=/var/lib/rook/rook-ceph/client.admin.keyring --format json
2025-09-11 16:37:31.785810 E | cleanup: failed to add task to remove image "csi-vol-456f3d06-cbf2-44ee-bb7b-c3fa01d3a884" from trash in cephblockpool "my-pool" in rados namespace "my-namespace": failed to delete image "cf64122559b990" in cephblockpool "my-pool" from trash: exit status 11

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
